name: Higueruelas Trail
name_original: Aldeas de Higueruela – circular desde Santa Cruz de Moya (PR-CU 13)
description: Hike the difficult 17.0-mile Aldeas de Higueruela circular route from Santa Cruz de Moya, featuring pine forests and views.
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Cruz De Moya offers diverse routes through mountainous terrain in Spain's Cuenca province, bordering Valencia. The region is characterized by limestone mountain ranges, extensive pine forests, and the meandering River Turia, which creates picturesque riparian landscapes. Situated within the Alto Turia Biosphere Reserve, the area provides broad mountain panoramas and a blend of natural features. Best hiking trails around Santa Cruz De Moya The most popular hiking route is Water Trail, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 2 minutes to complete. This circular route features water elements and views of the Turia River. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Sierra Trail, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This route includes viewpoints such as Mirador del Pulpitejo. Local hikers also love the Los Rubiales - Rio Turia (PR-CV 187) — Circular desde Aras de los Olmos, an 11.0 miles (17.8 km) trail leading through the Turia River area, often completed in about 5 hours 28 minutes. Hiking around Santa Cruz De Moya is defined by limestone mountain ranges, extensive pine forests, and the River Turia. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Santa Cruz De Moya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 18 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Cruz De Moya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Santa Cruz De Moya?
A: The hiking trails in Santa Cruz De Moya traverse a mountainous landscape characterized by limestone mountain ranges and extensive pine forests. You'll also encounter picturesque riparian areas along the Turia River, which flows through the region, offering a blend of forest and riverside scenery.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in Santa Cruz De Moya?
A: There are nearly 30 hiking routes available in Santa Cruz De Moya, catering to various skill levels. This includes 8 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 5 more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, Santa Cruz De Moya offers several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010570743/water-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.3 km loop featuring water elements and views of the Turia River. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822450801/the-forest-of-las-blancas-circular-from-puebla-de-san-miguel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Forest of Las Blancas — Circular from Puebla de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.4 km hike.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see on the trails?
A: Many trails offer broad mountain panoramas and opportunities to experience the region's natural beauty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010289864/la-sierra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sierra Trail&lt;/a&gt; includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Pulpitejo&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Blancas (Juniper Forest)&lt;/a&gt; or visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicente Pine&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more demanding experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010577208/higueruelas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Higueruelas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.3 km route. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822504358/los-rubiales-rio-turia-pr-cv-187-circular-desde-aras-de-los-olmos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Rubiales - Rio Turia (PR-CV 187)&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 17.8 km through the Turia River area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Santa Cruz De Moya?
A: The hiking routes in Santa Cruz De Moya are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 18 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the picturesque Turia River, and the extensive pine forests that define the area's natural charm.

Q: Can I find trails that feature water elements or rivers?
A: Yes, the Turia River is a prominent natural feature in Santa Cruz De Moya, and several trails incorporate its presence. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010570743/water-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Trail&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to highlight water elements and offers beautiful views of the river. During summer, the river also provides opportunities for bathing.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several interesting sites. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5707485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of Moya&lt;/a&gt; for a glimpse into local history, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4599624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dino Route of Aras de los Olmos&lt;/a&gt; for a unique natural monument experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Buey Shelter&lt;/a&gt; also offers a point of interest for hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Cruz De Moya?
A: Santa Cruz De Moya is appealing for outdoor activities due to its mountainous terrain. While specific seasonal conditions can vary, the region's natural features, including the Turia River, make it particularly enjoyable during warmer months when you can also take advantage of bathing opportunities. Always check local weather forecasts before your hike.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, Santa Cruz De Moya offers 8 easy routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. These trails provide an accessible way to enjoy the region's natural beauty without significant difficulty.

Q: What makes Santa Cruz De Moya a unique hiking destination?
A: Santa Cruz De Moya stands out due to its location within the Alto Turia Biosphere Reserve, offering significant ecological and scenic value. The blend of limestone mountain ranges, extensive pine forests, and the picturesque Turia River creates a diverse and immersive hiking experience, combining natural beauty with a sense of historical memory and traditional mountain architecture.
tips: From the El Vallecillo spot there are beautiful views when approaching the small town of Higueruela. Coming from Santa Cruz de Moya, the trail runs through a very picturesque area of the province of Cuenca, between limestone mountains, pine forests and some farms.

In this place known as Las Simas there is a spring of water that has supplied the towns in the area with water for many years until its salinity level increased and it was no longer used for this purpose, becoming a very picturesque tourist place.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Aldeas de Higueruela circular trail?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular route is approximately 27.3 kilometers long. It typically takes around 7 hours and 57 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is the Aldeas de Higueruela circular trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as a difficult hike (senderismo difícil). It requires a very good level of physical fitness and sure-footedness due to its length, elevation gain of about 700 meters, and varied terrain. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Aldeas de Higueruela circular?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows ancient mule tracks (caminos de herradura) with some shorter sections of unpaved tracks. You'll traverse calcareous clay soils between mountains, with generally gentle slopes. Appropriate footwear is essential.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience picturesque landscapes characteristic of the Cuenca province, including limestone mountain ranges, dense pine forests, and scattered farms. Notable natural features include the Hoz del Turia (gorges of the Turia River). As you approach Higueruela, you'll get beautiful views of the village from areas like El Vallecillo.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or highlights along the Aldeas de Higueruela circular?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty, the route incorporates cultural and historical elements. You'll pass by sites such as the Torrecilla archaeological site, Cueva El Tesoro, old livestock corrals and caves, the Higueruelas springs, and the Casa del Marqués. Don't miss the Views of Higueruela and the natural monument Las Simas.

faqs.question: Does the Aldeas de Higueruela circular trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows the Sendero de Higueruelas for about 91% of its length. It also briefly intersects with sections of the Sendero del Agua and passes through parts of Calle Altavista and Calle Higueruelas.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Aldeas de Higueruela circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Santa Cruz de Moya. While specific parking details are not provided, you would typically find parking available within or on the outskirts of Santa Cruz de Moya, as it serves as the main access point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Aldeas de Higueruela circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The information available does not specify restrictions against dogs. However, as a general rule for challenging hikes in natural areas, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the presence of diverse wildlife and potential livestock. Please ensure your dog is also fit enough for a nearly 8-hour hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Aldeas de Higueruela circular?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, given the trail's location in Cuenca and its length, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in this region. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is often at its best. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder conditions or even snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail, especially since it's in Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the broader region of Serranía de Cuenca. While there are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for hiking the PR-CU 13 itself, it's always advisable to respect general regulations for natural parks. This typically includes staying on marked trails, not disturbing wildlife, and practicing Leave No Trace principles. Check local park authority websites for any specific rules or advisories before your visit.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Santa Cruz de Moya?
faqs.answer: Santa Cruz de Moya is a small village and serves as the starting point for this route. While specific facilities are not detailed, it's common for such villages to have basic amenities like a local bar, cafe, or guesthouse. It's recommended to check in advance for availability, especially if you plan to stay overnight or rely on services before or after your hike.
